2603.20510.title: Grounded Chess Reasoning in Language Models via Master Distillation
2603.20510.paper_summary: Language models often lack grounded reasoning capabilities in specialized domains where training data is scarce but bespoke systems excel. We introduce a general framework for distilling expert system reasoning into natural language chain-of-thought explanations, enabling compact models to acquire domain expertise and the ability to generate faithful, grounded explanations. Rather than distilling only final outputs, we capture the full reasoning process, transforming opaque expert computations into transparent, step-by-step explanations. We demonstrate this approach in chess, a canonical reasoning domain where language models continue to underperform. Our 4B parameter model, C1, advances from a near-zero baseline to 48.1% accuracy, outperforming all open-source models and most frontier proprietary systems. Notably, C1 surpasses its distillation teacher and generates solutions in two orders of magnitude fewer tokens than baselines. Unlike prior neural chess approaches that predict only best moves, C1 generates explainable solutions revealing strategic reasoning. Our pipeline combines supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning with theme-balanced data sampling for comprehensive tactical coverage. Master Distillation demonstrates how to inject expert-level knowledge into compact models for under-optimized domains, offering a recipe for unlocking RLVR where LLMs lack sufficient base capabilities.
2603.20510.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型在專業領域中常常缺乏扎根推理能力，這些領域的訓練數據稀缺，而定制系統則表現出色。<lb/>我們提出了一個通用框架，用於將專家系統的推理提煉成自然語言的思維鏈解釋，使得緊湊模型能夠獲得領域專業知識和生成真實、扎根的解釋能力。<lb/>我們不僅僅提煉最終輸出，而是捕捉完整的推理過程，將不透明的專家計算轉化為透明的逐步解釋。<lb/>我們在國際象棋這一經典推理領域展示了這一方法，因為語言模型在這方面仍然表現不佳。<lb/>我們的4B參數模型C1，從接近零的基線提升至48.1%的準確率，超越了所有開源模型和大多數前沿專有系統。<lb/>值得注意的是，C1超越了其提煉教師，並在生成解決方案時使用的標記數量比基線少兩個數量級。<lb/>與以往僅預測最佳走法的神經象棋方法不同，C1生成可解釋的解決方案，揭示了戰略推理。<lb/>我們的管道結合了監督微調和強化學習，並通過主題平衡數據抽樣實現全面的戰術覆蓋。<lb/>大師提煉展示了如何將專家級知識注入緊湊模型，以應對未優化的領域，提供了一個解鎖RLVR的配方，因為LLMs缺乏足夠的基礎能力。
2603.20510.author: Zhenwei Tang et.al.
2603.20510.authors: Zhenwei Tang, Qianfeng Wen, Seth Grief-Albert, Yahya Elgabra, Blair Yang, Honghua Dong, Ashton Anderson
